methods.description: Process for preparing clay samples by removing organic matter and calcium carbonate, followed by separation of clay fractions for analysis.

steps.description: The air-dry samples were treated with H2O2 to remove organic matter and dilute HCl to remove the calcium carbonate.
steps.searchable_summary: treat samples with H2O2 and HCl to remove organic matter and calcium carbonate

steps.description: The clay fractions were separated by gravity separation using Stoke's Law.
steps.searchable_summary: separate clay fractions using gravity separation and Stoke's Law

methods.description: XRD analysis of clay samples to identify and quantify clay minerals under different conditions.

steps.description: XRD analysis of the oriented mounts was carried out for each sample under natural (air-dried) conditions (N), ethylene-glycol solvation for 24 h in a desiccator (EG), and heating at 550°C for 2 h (H).
steps.searchable_summary: perform XRD analysis under different conditions

steps.description: XRD patterns were obtained using a PANalytical X'Pert Pro MPD diffractometer with CuKα radiation and Ni filter, under a voltage of 40 kV and a current of 40 mA.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain XRD patterns using PANalytical X'Pert Pro MPD diffractometer

steps.description: The scans were performed from 3°to 30°, with a step size of 0.0167°.
steps.searchable_summary: perform XRD scans from 3° to 30° with step size 0.0167°

steps.description: A high resolution scan from 24°to 26°2θ at a rate of 0.1°2θ per minute was carried out to isolate the contributions of kaolinite and chlorite in the 3.5 Å peak.
steps.searchable_summary: perform high resolution XRD scan from 24° to 26°2θ

steps.description: Semi-quantitative analysis of clay minerals were determined from the basal reflection peak areas.
steps.searchable_summary: determine semi-quantitative analysis from basal reflection peak areas

steps.description: The relative abundances of clay minerals were determined using this formula: 4×I(illite 10 Å) + I(smectite 17 Å) + 2×I(kaolinite, chlorite 7 Å) = 100%.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate relative abundances of clay minerals using given formula
